assertion_results.text: Data extraction correctly identifies CVE-2026-31812, library quinn-proto, affected range < 0.11.14, and fixed version 0.11.14 from the Vulnerability issue (Step 1)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md Step 1 table: CVE ID = CVE-2026-31812, Vulnerable library = quinn-proto, Affected version range = 'versions before 0.11.14 (< 0.11.14)', Fixed version = 0.11.14

assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as Cargo based on the library name and component context (Step 1 Ecosystem detection)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md Ecosystem Detection section: 'The vulnerable library `quinn-proto` is a Rust crate. Ecosystem: **Cargo**.' Also confirms Cargo.lock as the lock file and lists ecosystem mappings for both streams.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the security-matrix.md supportability matrix — no versions are skipped (Important Rule 4)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md table includes all 7 versions: 2.1.0 (v0.3.8), 2.1.1 (v0.3.12), 2.2.0 (v0.4.5), 2.2.1 (v0.4.8), 2.2.2 (v0.4.9), 2.2.3 (v0.4.11), 2.2.4 (v0.4.12). This matches the full set of versions consistently referenced across all evals.

assertion_results.text: Version impact analysis uses pinned commit tags from the supportability matrix for each version, not branch HEAD (Important Rule 13)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md Build Tag column contains specific pinned tags for each version: v0.3.8, v0.3.12, v0.4.5, v0.4.8, v0.4.9, v0.4.11, v0.4.12. These are per-version pinned tags, not branch HEAD references.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table identifies retag versions and carries forward the affected status from the original version (Important Rule 5)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md row for 2.2.2: 'v0.4.9 | 0.11.12 | YES | retag of 2.2.1 (same backend commit as v0.4.8)'. Retag is identified and affected status (YES) is carried forward from 2.2.1.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction proposes replacing PSIRT-assigned versions with lock-file-verified versions, showing the diff (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md shows the diff: 'Current Affects Versions:  [RHTPA 2.0.0]' -> 'Proposed Affects Versions: [RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1, RHTPA 2.2.2]'. Rationale explains RHTPA 2.0.0 is incorrect and the correction is based on lock file analysis at pinned commits.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction references version names from the supportability matrix rather than hardcoded Jira version IDs (Important Rule 6)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md uses version names (RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1, RHTPA 2.2.2) throughout the diff and rationale. The Jira mutation block uses placeholders '<jira-id-for-RHTPA-2.2.0>' and explicitly states: 'Jira version IDs would be discovered dynamically via `getJiraIssueTypeMetaWithFields` (Step 3.1) -- not hardcoded.'

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks include Repository, Target Branch, acceptance criteria, and dependency linkage between upstream and downstream tasks (Important Rule 9)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task 1: Repository = backend, Target Branch = release/0.4.z, Acceptance Criteria with 3 checkboxes, linked to TC-8001 via Depend. Task 2: Repository = rhtpa-release.0.4.z, Target Branch = main, Acceptance Criteria with 2 checkboxes, linked via Blocks to upstream task and Depend to TC-8001.

assertion_results.text: For Cargo ecosystem, remediation creates two tasks: upstream backport task and downstream propagation subtask with Blocks dependency (Important Rule 8)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md creates Task 1 (Upstream Backport 2.2.x) and Task 2 (Downstream Propagation 2.2.x). Task 2 linkage includes: 'jira.create_link(inwardIssue: "<upstream-task-key>", outwardIssue: "<downstream-task-key>", type: "Blocks")'

assertion_results.text: The analysis presents triage recommendations as proposed actions — framing Affects Versions changes, label additions, and status transitions as proposals rather than executed mutations (Guardrails)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md section header: '## Jira Mutation (pending engineer confirmation)'. affects-versions.md comment section: '## Comment (pending engineer confirmation)'. remediation.md uses proposed linkage syntax without claiming execution. Data-extraction.md Step 0.7 uses descriptive language like 'was previously Unassigned. Now assigned' without claiming executed mutations in eval mode.

assertion_results.text: The data-extraction output describes Step 0.7 actions before the Step 1 data table: assigning the CVE issue to the current user and transitioning it to Assigned status (Step 0.7)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md starts with '# Step 0.7 -- Assign and Transition to Assigned' section describing: (1) retrieve current user account ID, (2) assign TC-8001 to current user, (3) discover target transition dynamically, (4) transition TC-8001 from New to Assigned. This section appears before '# Step 1 -- Data Extraction' which follows on line 44.
